Issue: Fix `AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'numel'` when `RandGridDistortiond` skips the transform (random probability not met)
Decision: Convert only the keyed tensor items via `self.key_iterator(d)` instead of calling `convert_to_tensor(d, ...)` on the entire data dict
Rationale: Root cause: `convert_to_tensor(d, ...)` was called on the entire data dict, which fails when non-tensor metadata (ints, strings) is present. Fix ensures consistency with how other dict transforms handle the no-transform path by converting only keyed tensor items.
Author: HeyangQin
Description: RandGridDistortiond transform in MONAI library, specifically addressing the no-transform path when random probability is not met
Cost: Low (simple code change with targeted fix and minimal scope)
Risk: Technical debt: Potential side effects from modifying the no-transform path. Risk of introducing new bugs in edge cases (e.g., DataLoader with num_workers>0). Uncertainty: Integration testing pending for DataLoader scenario.

Argument.author: HeyangQin
Argument.argument: Fixing the no-transform path by converting only keyed tensor items (`self.key_iterator(d)`) is better than calling `convert_to_tensor(d, ...)` on the entire data dict because the latter fails when non-tensor metadata (ints, strings) is present.

Argument.author: HeyangQin
Argument.argument: The fix ensures consistency with how other dict transforms handle the no-transform path, avoiding crashes when the transform is skipped due to random probability not being met.

Justification: The issue involves fixing a transform (RandGridDistortiond) in the MONAI library, which is part of data preprocessing. The error (`AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'numel'`) occurs when non-tensor metadata (ints, strings) is present during data loading, implying a data ingestion/preparation problem rather than modeling or deployment.
